reasoning:
The historical base rate for ACLED "Battles" in Bosnia and Herzegovina is roughly 1 per year (derived from the 0.083 average per month on 2025-10-16). The single event in the past year was likely the May 2025 shootout between rival smuggler gangs near a migrant camp. We therefore estimate an ~8.3% monthly baseline probability for at least one battle. 

For the first resolution date (2025-11-02), the 30-day window is Oct 4 - Nov 2. As of the forecast date (Oct 26), 23 days have elapsed. Exhaustive local and international searches found no evidence of any armed clashes, shootouts, or other battle-like events during this period. The base rate for the remaining 7 days is mathematically ~2%. Accounting for potential reporting lags and the required lower bound, we estimate 5%. 

For the second date (2025-11-25), the 30-day window (Oct 27 - Nov 25) is entirely in the future. We rely on the base rate (~8.3%) and assign 9%. 

For the third date (2026-01-24), representing another future 30-day window (Dec 26 - Jan 24), the baseline remains similar. Following the instruction to increase uncertainty and pull towards 0.5 for more distant horizons, we increase the confidence interval and estimate 14%.
